description: Cross-language conformance vectors for the ShieldChannel session-key derivation and the underlying PAKEExchange primitives. Every binding MUST reproduce these values byte-for-byte. This anchors the channel/PAKE construction the same way tests/v4_test_vectors.json anchors the base AEAD and tests/pq_kex_vectors.json anchors the PQ KEX. Without it, EVERY non-Rust binding silently diverged from the Rust source of truth (Go/Python/JS/Java/C#/Kotlin/Android/Swift all used plain SHA256 in PAKEExchange.derive/combine instead of keyed HMAC-SHA256); the base session-key step alone was aligned on 2026-06-30 but the primitives were not, so the channel remained non-interoperable and no test caught it.
construction: base_key            = HMAC-SHA256(sorted[0], sorted[1] || ...)  where sorted = sort(contributions)   [PAKEExchange.combine]
session_key         = HMAC-SHA256(base_key, derived_key || utf8(service))   [truncated to 32 bytes]

pake_primitives.comment: Direct anchors for PAKEExchange.derive/combine so bindings without a ShieldChannel (C#, Java, Kotlin, Swift) can still verify their primitives.
